expectations.text: Scopes the analysis by checking recent work and progress-tracker before broad reading
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows executor ran `git log --oneline -30` and read `architect-workspace` before reading any context files. response.md Scoping section explicitly documents: 'Scoped analysis to the recently completed work: billing settings page and InvoiceStatusBadge reusable component. Checked git history (recent commits show billing module additions), reviewed progress-tracker.md (billing items were listed as pending), and identified drift across progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, build-plan, and code-standards as the files most likely affected.'

expectations.text: Looks for drift in progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, and ui-rules as relevant
expectations.evidence: response.md 'Files Updated' section covers all 8 listed files: progress-tracker.md (moved billing items to completed), ui-registry.md (added InvoiceStatusBadge component entry), architecture.md (added billing module to structure and data model), build-plan.md (marked Phase 6 complete), code-standards.md (added badge variant and formatDate dependency rules), ui-tokens.md (added badge status color mappings), ui-rules.md (added InvoiceStatusBadge prop and accessibility rules), and library-docs.md (explicitly noted 'No changes needed. No new dependencies were introduced'). All 8 files exist on disk under outputs/project-root/context/.

expectations.text: Updates only context/*.md files and never changes source code or tests
expectations.evidence: Transcript shows all writes targeted outputs/project-root/context/*.md (8 files), outputs/response.md, and outputs/manifest.json. The final git status check (transcript line 79) confirmed 'no tracked files modified. Only untracked new files in the eval/skill directories.' No source code (.tsx, .ts) or test files were created or modified.

expectations.text: Does not pre-document features that do not exist
expectations.evidence: All context files document only the two features stated as completed in the prompt: the billing settings page and InvoiceStatusBadge component. Future features (subscription management, invoice history, tax config, payment webhooks) remain listed as pending in progress-tracker.md and build-plan.md. No hallucinated features were documented as complete.

expectations.text: Preserves existing context-file structure and conventions
expectations.evidence: All 8 context files maintain their established structure: heading + Last Updated date, followed by domain-appropriate sections using tables and bullet lists. New entries follow existing patterns exactly — e.g., ui-registry.md InvoiceStatusBadge row uses the same Component/File/Token Usage/Notes table columns as existing entries; architecture.md billing module follows the same module description and boundary pattern; code-standards.md adds rules in the same bullet-list convention format; ui-tokens.md Badge Status Colors table mirrors the existing Color Tokens table style.

expectations.text: Reports files updated, drift detected but not fixed, and an overall verdict
expectations.evidence: response.md contains all three required sections: (1) 'Files Updated' lists 7 updated files with specific change descriptions plus library-docs.md as unchanged; (2) 'Drift Detected But Not Fixed' identifies 3 specific drift items — invoices table schema inference, billing module folder structure assumptions, and the fact that no source code was actually read; (3) 'Verdict' states 'Minor drift' with recommendation for manual review of architecture.md data model and ui-registry.md file paths. manifest.json corroborates with files_updated, files_inspected_unchanged, drift_items, and verdict fields.

claims.claim: The executor checked git history to scope the analysis
claims.evidence: Transcript shows `git log --oneline -30` executed before any context files were read

claims.claim: All 8 context files were inspected and their drift status assessed
claims.evidence: All 8 files exist under outputs/project-root/context/; response.md documents the status of each

claims.claim: 7 context files were updated, 1 (library-docs) was left unchanged
claims.evidence: manifest.json files_updated lists 7 entries; files_inspected_unchanged lists library-docs.md; library-docs.md content confirms no billing-specific additions

claims.claim: Badge color mappings are consistent across ui-tokens.md, ui-registry.md, and architecture.md
claims.evidence: All three files agree: paid→--color-success/#16a34a, pending→--color-warning/#d97706, overdue→--color-danger/#dc2626, void→--color-muted/#6b7280

claims.claim: The executor simulated the skill rather than running it against a real codebase
claims.evidence: Transcript line 48: 'This is a sandboxed eval — no actual project codebase exists. The syncdocs skill would normally scan git, context files, and source code. I need to simulate the skill's behavior producing realistic outputs.' The drift items in response.md also acknowledge this: 'all context file contents are reconstructed from the skill's understanding of what the billing features would contain based on the prompt.'

suggestions.reason: All 6 assertions pass at 1.0, but the executor explicitly acknowledged it was simulating the skill against a fake codebase ('no actual project codebase exists... I need to simulate the skill's behavior'). This means the assertions verify output format compliance, not that the skill actually scanned real files and detected real drift. A stronger eval would provide a minimal real project scaffold so the skill has actual files to scan and drift to detect — otherwise these assertions are trivially satisfiable by any model that can produce plausible-looking context files.

suggestions.assertion: Does not pre-document features that do not exist
suggestions.reason: This assertion is trivially satisfied in a sandbox with no real codebase — there is nothing to check against. In a real project eval, this would require comparing documented features against actual source code to verify nothing was fabricated.

suggestions.assertion: Looks for drift in progress-tracker, ui-registry, architecture, code-standards, library-docs, build-plan, ui-tokens, and ui-rules as relevant
suggestions.reason: No assertion checks the quality or correctness of the drift detection itself. The executor reported 'No changes needed' for library-docs.md, but a real syncdocs run might have found drift there too (e.g., if a new date-fns dependency was added). Consider adding an assertion that verifies at least one file was correctly left unchanged for a substantive reason, not just because no new deps were mentioned in the prompt.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions check structural compliance well but cannot verify skill behavior against real code. The sandbox setup makes every assertion passable by simulation alone. The strongest improvement would be providing a minimal project scaffold with intentional drift so the skill must detect and fix real discrepancies.
